# AKS proof - 2026-06-19T15-22-28Z

- Commit: `54eab677e203f11d7759002f3409dfb75d256c60` (image tag `latest`)
- App URL: https://aks-newcode.msulawiak.pl  (/healthz HTTP `200`)
- Metrics URL: https://grafana-newcode.msulawiak.pl  (/api/health HTTP `200`)
- Window: this cluster is left RUNNING; the next scheduled `down` (45 min after each `up`, or manual) tears it down. The always-on front stays up.

```
NAME                 DATA   AGE
cv-site-prometheus   1      54s
cv-site-grafana-provisioning   2     55s
cv-site-grafana-dashboards   1     56s

==> v1/Service
NAME              TYPE        CLUSTER-IP   EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)    AGE
cv-site-metrics   ClusterIP   None         <none>        9113/TCP   55s
cv-site-prometheus   ClusterIP   10.0.50.159   <none>   9090/TCP   55s
cv-site-grafana   ClusterIP   10.0.173.228   <none>   3000/TCP   55s
cv-site   ClusterIP   10.0.100.24   <none>   80/TCP,9113/TCP   55s


TEST SUITE: None
NOTES:
cv-site latest deployed to namespace cv (release: cv-site).

Architecture recap:
  * The site is served by nginx on :8080 behind a ClusterIP Service.
  * Ingress is OUTBOUND-ONLY: 2x cloudflared dial the Cloudflare edge.
    The cluster has ZERO inbound ports open to the internet.
  * The tunnel token is read from Key Vault (kv-newcode-cv) via the
    Secrets Store CSI Driver + Workload Identity. No secrets in this repo.

Verify rollout:
  kubectl -n cv rollout status deploy/cv-site
  kubectl -n cv rollout status deploy/cv-site-cloudflared

Check health from inside the cluster (API server is PRIVATE; use the tailnet):
  kubectl -n cv port-forward svc/cv-site 8080:80
  curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/healthz
Metrics: nginx-prometheus-exporter on :9113 is scraped by the
ServiceMonitor into Azure Managed Prometheus -> Grafana RED dashboard.

Public URL: https://aks.newcode.msulawiak.pl (live only while the cluster is up).

Tenant isolation demo namespaces:
  * tenant-a (default-deny ingress + egress)
  * tenant-b (default-deny ingress + egress)
```
